reviews.review_text:
As community-friendly and accredited as this game is, I recently came across quite possibly the worst consumer scam in the video game market when I was trying to get back into Cities Skylines. 

As a preface, I switched to console gaming a long time ago. So when I stopped using my computer, I downloaded Cities Skylines on my PS4. Over a period of about 4-5 ish weeks, I put money aside to buy DLC until I had it all. 
Mind you, Paradox's model is literally DLC centered! Nearly all of their games cost 100-400 dollars in just DLC! They literally have a SUBSCRIPTION for DLC on nearly ALL OF THEIR GAMES!!!

When you load the Paradox launcher to play Cities Skylines, there is a text bubble that f**king says "LOG INTO YOUR PARADOX ACCOUNT TO ACCESS ALL OF YOUR DLC" 

Imagine my frustration when I log into my Paradox account on my pc... And DONT have access to the DLC I spent weeks putting money aside for!!!

Blew the dust off the Playstation and sure enough, I'm signed into my Paradox account there. So I look it up online, and people are literally being MOCKED for expecting cross platform DLC when you are literally F***ING PROMPTED to sign into a CROSS PLATFORM PARADOX ACCOUNT!???! 

YOUR BUSINESS MODEL IS CENTERED AROUND DLC. YOU HAVE A CROSS PLATFORM DATA STORAGE SYSTEM. 

To assume that this "lack" of a feature is something less than greedy, malicious, noncompliance that f***s over the dedicated players who are willing to drop paychecks on your games would mean that you're willing to repaint your living room the same color twice. That is absolutely absurd.

DEAD BY DAYLIGHT, A GAME THAT ISNT EVEN SUPPORTED ANYMORE HAS THIS FEATURE. 

YOU DON'T SAVE MY MAPS, YOU DON'T SAVE MY PROGRESS, YOU DON'T REMEMBER WHAT I SPENT MY MONEY ON, WHY ARE YOU GIVING ME THE OPTION TO SIGN IN ON BOTH PLATFORMS, THEN TELLING ME ITS SO I CAN LOAD MY DLC. 

Go to hell.

Good game, BUT!

+ + + A very good city builder/designer. Work much better than Cities: Skylines 2. 
It is more of a "city painter" where you can design a nice city without much depth apart from traffic management. If you want REAL CHALLENGE, go for Workers and Resources: Soviet Republic. Less pretty but way more complex. 

- - - Note that 70% of the content what this game can to offer is locked behind DLC paywalls. OVER 100€ WORTH OF DLC ON SALE.... Lates DLC is 12€... and has issues.  Alternative option is the monthly sub for a 11 years old game. 
Too much greed and not enough focus on the actual development of the gameplay.

One comment I was reading in a forum and completely agree with:
""I have the impression that the Paradox game developers are under a strong constant pressure to add new features into the game, so that Paradox can sell more DLC. Due to this, Paradox games are constantly being flooded with large amounts of half-baked new features, which is hurting the core gameplay of its games. I really wish that Paradox would spend more time polishing existing features and fixing bugs, and would be more careful about adding new features to the game, because these new features often upset the delicate balance of the game.""

- Get the base game on sale and if you like it then add some DLC you like.
But be aware, this game is designed that the more you play the more DLC content you wanna see in your city.
Moddig is a good option to avoid paid DLC packages but note that updates will break them.

A very, rich and deep game to sink thousands of hours into. The workshop is also god tier and a transportation and architectural nerds dream. Cities: Skylines is the most satisfying experiance when you want to relax, tinker, problem solve and design. I'm 4498 hours in and still learning new ways to do things. Each new city i build i do so having learnt from previious cities mistakes and lessons learned until one day i'll master my ultimate city. 10/10
You play without mods - a game of crap , you play with mods - the game takes out incompatible mods - crap 10/10

Has been a fantastic game for so long until the last update 'race day' which essentially destroyed by 6 year old project I am unlikely to finish, as it is heavily modded and broke the legacy mods I had relied upon which survived so many older updates. All just to get some more money out of the player base after many many DLC's. RIP all the time I spent creating it. Only a miracle can save it. As it stands i do not recommend this game, before the last patch i would have. Best move to cities skylines 2 at this point which has seen some good improvement.

La verdad gran juego. pero el problema del juego es que auque compres todos los dlc el juego sigue siendo una mierda, la unica forma de complementar bien el gameplay es con mods de la workshop 

vale la pena comprarse todos los DLC? NO! vale la pena subscribirse para jugar? SI!
aun asi el trafico en juego sera una mierda XD jasdja
good game, but unmodded is not really great. also DLCs super expensive and many core features are in those DLCs
Installing it after a long time away, it now comes with spyware.  If you opt out of data collection you cannot play the game.

I've played this game on and off for a long time. But it's only now that I feel compelled to actually critique it. Because I'm probably going to uninstall it, and send it to the hidden section of my library.

Let me be frank. If you are going to build American-style stroad cities that are primarily suburbs. It might be an ok experience. 

But if your goal is to build an actual competent city with density and mass transit....do yourself a favor and save your money. So much of this game is locked behind DLC. I don't have much to say there that has not already been said. 

But it seems as though this game was originally developed with the core assumption that the disaster that is American urbanism is the ideal. Given the developers were European I am not sure why they ever thought that was a good idea to build a city-planning game around. Maybe they never actually visited the United States in person and only ever consumed movies that were romantic about Suburbia or whatever. The point is that this game rapidly breaks and glitches if you actually set out to design a real city. 

 This game being written on Unity was already a red flag. The baked in assumptions are nails in the coffin.

Unless you use a bunch of mods to force changes to AI behavior, remove limits on network and road shapes (they break constantly otherwise), and more. This game is going to raise your blood pressure to dangerous levels. Perfectly viable bus or metro routes get a mysterious silent refusal that can only be overcome with Network Anarchy. Death waves have no clear cause. And even when traffic is not the bottleneck, buildings will get saturated with garbage and nothing will change. Which is especially infuriating when the only solutions to bulldoze or reset end up nuking the progress in a University or completely chowdering a transit system's lines. Just naming a few. This game's dysfunctions are many.

When playing I felt like I was rarely struggling with actual engineering, physics, and logistical issues. But instead I feel like I am negotiating a minefield laid by poor development.

It's a bummer that there really is no competition in the City Planner genre of simulation. And it shows with the disaster that is this game's successor. 

Overall, this game might be fun for a time before its inherent dysfunction catches up with a vengeance. Then it's just a quagmire of bugfixing, glitches, and performance problems.

 Not only was the original development team incompetent. But they chose to build this game with an engine that at best is a jack of all that does nothing particularly well. But more commonly flatlines in subtle, infuriating ways because it simply cannot scale reliably beyond a toy demo.

No, I won't be absolving the Colossal Order devs here. The fairy godmother did not write the code, nor design the baked in systems. The development team is equally as complicit in this game's dysfunction as Paradox Interactive. A bad publisher is merely a force multiplier. 

4/10: Would not recommend beyond an occasional curiosity play. If and when a proper city-planner game comes along the Skylines games need to be discarded by all.
Too complicated for my tiny brain but its probably a good game.

I'm not going to lie, I'm over 100 hours in and I barely understand how this game works. I've managed to make exactly one successful megacity that didn't end up going bankrupt, and I still don't know how I did it. The technical aspects of this game are amazing, but inscrutable. Mostly I just turn off money and build. 10/10, no notes.

Very fun to play and design cities. It has a steep learning curve and can become frustrating at first, But keep with it and you will learn how to use to tools to design exactly what you want or need. The game also encourages you to pay attention to your own city/town or research other cities to get ideas for how to design your city!
9/10 with mods, like a 7 without mods imo, overall experience is fun if ur creative.
This is a very engaging game that deals with building and growing your virtual city

I love this game and I have been wanting to play it since I was young . It΄s very addicting and sophisticated , especially when you want to recreate a city from real life, that΄s what I have been doing mostly.. On the other hand , its hardware is pretty limiting in lots of areas and gets frustrating to play sometimes ,also the dlc is a little ridiculous since you have to spend lots of money to get a better experience... Mods kind of save this but not with eveything . Besides its flaws ,this game is an excellent choice for someone that wants to build their own  city or recreate one  from real life . You΄re gonna spend many hours playing this game ,trust me.....
Basically the best game ever infinite playtime and amazing :)

Really solid game. Unlike most city builders, this one focuses heavily on logistics and infrastructure. You will spend a lot of time looking at traffic, import/export routes, highway connections, etc. It sounds boring at first, but it actually leads to a game which does a great job at combining a creative sandbox with puzzles and problem solving. On the one hand, you have extensive control on how your city looks, but on the other hand you need to make it actually functional. The result is a game where everything you do matters and has a practical purpose with functional considerations. Everything really comes to life, every choice you make has knock on effects which might be small at first but build to something greater over time. It doesnt feel like what you do is arbitrary, it feels like you are building a real city which is being used by real people. 

Another thing the game does really well is the actual designing process. The game has both deep macro and micro planning. Most of your city planning is not placing specific buildings, but its establishing different types of zones where buildings will then be built based on demand. You dont place a house, you say 'alright along this road is a residential zone' and then houses are built as people move in. But then, the game still has plenty of specific buildings for you to place as well, one of the DLCs adds in college campuses you can design for example. It gives you extensive control without requiring you to micromanage every little detail, and the city naturally grows and evolves on its own over time which is so satisfying to watch. 

The main issues with the game however are first with its DLC policy. There are a lot. They can be expensive. The subscription service actually isnt a bad deal though, so consider that after learning how the game plays. Another issue is that there arent really any in game resources for learning, sure the game will teach you basic mechanics and how to play but youll find yourself having to do a lot of research outside of the game to learn how to make efficient road networks and such. The game also doesnt do a good job at communicating what issues are present, only that an issue is, so you have to do a lot of your own detective work to figure out how to fix things. On one hand, it encourages a more creative and open ended approach instead of just telling you what to do, but on the other, it can be obtuse to understand
This is a very fun game that i love playing cause it calms the nerves and allows my creative side to come out.
Great game. But without support from the mod community, the resident pathfinding AI doesn't work very well.

The attention to detail is quite insane!  The game allows you to let your creative mind out and lets you do pretty much anything you can image. There's many Mods/PLC to add plus the community sharing their own different mods. Its probably one of my favorite games, especially if you like engineering, designing, financials (if you wish), and problem solving. I've also haven't experienced any lagging, or graphic problems too.
I've only played the base game so far, but I really enjoy getting to build a city. I have started different save files and feel like I learn something new each round.

Spent more time fighting the paradox launcher ... finally blocked that spyware only to hate the UI, sound effects and ... well pretty much everting. Having grown up with Sim City, this game confuses me ... not the concept but the cartoon graphics style and the default settings for sound (awful music and all that clicking) ... and the push towards connecting online,  end especially that blue bird thing!!!  I cannot be bothered with the UI friction these choices have made. There may be some real complexity in a game from this generation (cf Sim City) ... but it feels like it was designed for a much younger audience.
I liked the small details and the depth that the game goes into realistic services provided by local governments.
I have 253.1 hours total throughout Steam and Xbox. Great game to kill time. A LOT better on Steam than Xbox due to workshop access.

This is a satisfying city building game. Still looks pretty decent and I'm sure there are plenty of mods. Appreciate that it still has developer support and hasn't been bloated, there is a lot of dlc but i've been happy with the base game. 

Always really appreciated the depth of the mechanics, only problem is coming back to the game after years it took a couple of hours to get the hang of everything again.

Don't think there's been in big city sim games since? I think these guys just won the genre for over a decade, definitely still worth your money

great game, verry addictive if your into it.
the graphics are a bit outdated but im not buying city skylines 2 annytime soon so for the time being is this still the best city builder all around.
I have literally become so addicted to this game it's negatively affecting my life and relationships; please play with caution

I have already played over 200 hours on Epic Games and still bought it again that's how goated this game is. Not only is it incredibly versatile, but it also encourages creativity. You can build almost anything you want it's like an engineer and an architect had a baby. The freedom it gives players is unmatched, and there are countless ways to approach every challenge. Overall, I give it a 10/10.

Good game, not worth paying for when CS2 is out.
It's got it's charm. CS2 has it's problems, but as does this game.
I read a review on CS2 that said "They are releasing DLCs for features that should have been out since release", and paradox being paradox does the same thing for this game as well.
Buy the new game, and if you want the dlc, uh, "figure something out".

The Ultimate and Most Immersive City-Building Experience!
After spending 65 hours crafting various cities from scratch, I can confidently say this is the absolute king of the city-builder genre. It offers so much more depth and satisfaction than any other city sim out there.

Deep and Detailed City Management: From zoning residential and industrial areas to managing water and power grids, every single micro-management mechanic is complex yet incredibly rewarding to master.

Challenging Traffic Simulation: The real boss in this game isn't a natural disaster—it's traffic congestion! Designing efficient roundabouts, highways, and public transit systems to keep the city moving perfectly is highly satisfying.

Endless Modding Potential: The Steam Workshop support gives this game infinite replay value. The community mods and assets are fantastic for polishing your city's aesthetics or adding awesome new mechanics.

A must-have for anyone who loves strategy, management, and urban planning. Highly addictive! 9.5/10.

So much better than Cities Skylines 2 (at the time of writing), the mods for this game are great and there are thousands of them. 
Warning: You will spend hours of your life solving traffic jams

I bought Cities: Skylines thinking I'd unwind for an hour after work. Oh boi was I wrong. I have not slept. I have, however, personally signed off on 47 zoning amendments, declared war on a single traffic intersection that has ruined my life, and developed a parasocial relationship with the shipping truck driver who keeps getting stuck on a roundabout I designed at 4 AM while making "just one more adjustment." My roommates ask where I've been. I told them I'm the mayor now. They just don't understand the responsibilities. The citizens of Sunhaven Bay need me. Their water treatment plant won't expand itself. 

10/10, please send help, also more highway interchange mods.
this game made me kill my entire family because the citizens kept getting sick. 10/10 game, would recommend, even if it got me arrested for life
great game...alot of support and community help and mods!
Traffic keeps adding up and blocking emergency and public service vehciles from reaching residences- causing them to be abandoned. 10/10
really fun game and it can be played even on lower end devices with integrated graphics

Bummer. This is going to suck. Why? Because I have friends right here on steam that stand by this product. Well, I cannot. Unfortunately, it's not the game designer's fault. This one sits squarely with Steam. All saves go to the steam cloud. All I wanted was to start over by erasing all of the saves for this game and start new. But I have no access. Ever try and get a hold of a tech at steam? Hahahaha - right. Good luck trying to find the path to leave them a message in the first place. When you do find it, you have to play an email back and forth game just to find out they "don't have any way of accessing it either". Yeah, it says I have only played for 8 hours. The frustration with the game play started way before the "save" thing started. Way to make a game more complicated than it needs to be... thus the restarting thing. Well, I won't be recommending this game because I never should have bought it in the first place. Why? Because all games that can only be saved on steam cloud does not allow you access. You think that would be put on the game listings somewhere in the requirements section so you could have that knowledge beforehand. Recommendation: Play CivCity Rome instead. It's cheap, your saves are on your own computer, and you don't need a mass listing of tutorials on YouTube to figure it out...
This game has made me lose track of time, building a city from scratch to a large population even though there are only 2 DLCs and a few mods, but it is really challenging.

We have been told three times now that a DLC will be the final release for this series.  I don't like being lied to, and I don't like you selling other people's work as your own.

You abandonded Cities 2 and now you want me to buy more DLC?  Yeah right, you'll be lucky if I ever buy another title from this publishet again! 

Assume that if you buy this game that it will be in a perpetual state of incompleteness.

Devs haven't added content to this game in years but have sold over a hundred dollars in add ons since sunsetting the game for the sequel. 

As a long time fan of the series and previously of the company that made it I just can't tolerate this much disrespect and still feel good about myself. 

Your companies policies and mistreatment of fans makes me sad about the state of the industry. 

You had several opportunities to make things right with the fans who had bought preorders and you screwed us over and over. 

You're hiding from the negative attention and I think it's unfair that this game is rated so highly when its success is also based on lies and broken promises like the sequel. 

I hope Iceflake is able to clean up your mess, but you're not getting any more money out of me.

You can forget the Stellaris DLC and Prison Architecht as well, you've showed your true colours now.
I love these city games. It has so much variety for design and realism.  The playthroughs on Youtube are so nice to watch.
Great replay value. I come back every 1-2 years to put in another 30 hours. 99% of the game is managing traffic, which is honestly more fun than it sounds.

The gameplay is absolutely amazing. If you ever played SimCity back in the day, this will feel familiar but fresh. It really shines as a city‑building experience, especially during the COVID era when it was one of the best games to dive into.

Some of the fun comes from buying DLCs, which can feel a bit necessary to unlock the full potential. Even so, the base game is strong enough to stand out and keep you hooked.

Final Score: 8/10
Best city builder on the market with a wonderful variety of mods and a very active community.

Awesome game, I love stalking my citizens and seeing their daily lives in the epic City i made them
100% worth getting if on sale
Absolutely fire of a game, im new to the city building genre and im already loving it, though have tried the second version it does not fit my style, still fire
its fun and relaxing but everything costs

its just a sick game, the community mods are what really keep me on, the quality of life mods are so good i cant play without them. This is a good game if you want to be stressed out all the time or if you just wanna chill. Its quite the learning curve, but its fun.
its a nice game as an introduction for management games. i would recommend it. easy to learn and easy to follow.
the game where you can cause diseases to your citizens due to untreated and unclean water, this is def peak

would be cool if they didnt destroy the game every few months with a 1kb update that makes every mod under the sun incompatible leaving your save unplayable. been fixing these issues for years and finally given up for good. game not worth it anymore, dont bother.

so recently i found my old computer but i couldnt find my mouse so ive had to use my xbox controller instead but its not compatible with this game and it should be cause its already on xbox and playstation i used to play the game on my xbox i cant give it a bad review cause this computer is windows 10 and 10+ years old

After a while the game gets a bit boring and I wish that if you had bought city skylines 1 you would get 2 for free. I suggest if  you want to buy the game just to immidietly buy number 2.
I love the game I've played on PC and Xbox and I'll say that i had more fun experiences with the pc. I'll admit too that the xbox version is still my favorite cause I got most of all the dlcs
The theme for this game is somehow relaxing , pausable , with regulated in game conflicts and  for long replayability for someone who doesn't much time in their hands

I would describe this game as a advanced Sim City. I love the UI and game details as it does have great features in the base game but I would say please get DLC's if you are looking for more detail, content and aesthetics.
Better & EASIER than SimCity, more fun, more customization, and overall a fun time!
its a great way to find out if your government is failing you by having you build your own and such

I have played this game for a long time... and I must say It is one of my favourites. Personally I really like city building or creating very detailed / nieche areas... Example: Airbase for this I don't think there is or will be any other game on the market that can simply have this many options in terms of building, it is truly where creativity flows and I would reccomend this game to anyone!

However I think this game is very very very enjoyable it has a lot of flaws which cs2 has mostly fixed but sometimes they can ruin your experience... visual bugs, playing without mods like anarchy can be a nightmare so I would reccomend playing with mods for the same creativity effect.... and like if you just want to play vanilla there is cs2

Sorry for bad english

I Love this Game!

It's addictive, amazing and your always able to add stuff! Albeit for some hefty prices. I believe this game is best for those with smooth running laptops or pcs. It's a bit complex to start but when you get the gist it's more easy than cutting a potato! If you do install make sure you have enough space because this game has a lot of traffic AI and also requires quite a bit of space. Good luck!
I've been playing this game months i enjoy it, runs fine on basically any PC. I'm building a city in Portugal and the steam workshop mods have made my map alot better.
Nothing about this game stood out to me, it's a decent city builder with some different systems you need to manage as the city grows which is fun at first, but the base game is overall uninteresting.

Recommend but wait for sale
Paradox asks for me to take out a second mortgage to play the full game but in the end this is probably the best city building sim ever made

I wanted to get this game for awhile due to seeing a good few youtubers playing and it seemed really interesting. When I was finally able to buy it a few days ago I was quite excited to play. Sadly, most of my time spent "playing" was constant pop up errors, horrible lag, crashing, freezing that continued AFTER I seemingly closed the game, and the game outright REFUSING to load maps or turn on/off mods.

All the hype I've been told about this game just for a product that doesn't even work and $32 of cash down the drain. What a waste of a game, and a waste of MY hard earned money on a product that is $32 more expensive than it should be.

♥♥♥♥♥ straight ass, you'll get a far better city sim game in MS-paint than here and at a FAR cheaper cost.
Good game. Less system demanding than CS:2, I believe. The graphics is great, cities skylines 1 is still relevant
THe best game ever perfect when you really get to understand layering and traffic flow and managing industry
i enjoy it, 100% pay for the base game and then the subscription, so worth it instead of buying everything individually.

great game, and runs well on most computers now even though its from 2015. while its incredible in vanilla, the modding is incredible it can make it look and feel like a completely different game, so many things you can do.
It's fun, it's relaxing, it's a nice game. Still having lots of fun in 2026 with Cities: Skylines. There is no better time than now to start the game and build the city of your dreams.

I have tortured my citizens in many ways but some include:

Making a poop tsunami that wipes out the city

Making the citizens drink their own waste by putting the water station right next to the sewer
Plus other ways that make my citizens want to kill themselves

Overall great game cause i can do anything with my city and the citizens
I like this game, inspite of its abhorrent design and mechanics
I liked about this product that it has been making me more productive. Also, I did get really interested in this game before getting it. It is the best game I ever tried.

mejor juego, lo tengo en mac mini y pfff, increible y con la suscripcion a todos los DLC siento que puedo hacer lo que sea en mis ciudades... AH!!! y por supuesto los mods que no podia yo usar en la version de windows o xbox
I love detailing I love problemsolving I love the challenge of combining excellent functioning with both realistic yet amazing visuals, the mods and extra content are still adding value to the game.

I'm writing this review quite some time after playing because I really wanted to get a feel of the the game to give a real review. I really enjoy Cities Skylines. I seem to lose track of time, every time I play lol I really like how I can let my creativity run wild with truly being able to create endless city designs and being able to change whatever I like simply adds to the fun!! I have full control over every little thing happening, from taxes to the street lights, it's 100% awesome... The graphics are great as well! I find myself zooming in and watching different parts of my city in awe and amazement, seriously such a great game! I would def recommend this game to anyone who enjoys a true city building and management simulation game.
This is a really detailed game with tons of potential. Pro tip: never use grids, they will always eventually fail.
nice and chill game a lil laggy but it pays off for the realism
Great and relaxing. kind lacking in content without dlc but other than that its great. runs good too
very god game bbecuzsd dthgnk about makgidsngh city in game siwth sandbox it sit very xcool

relaxing game to play until people just randomly start dying
10/10 i love building cityes

Cities:Skylines has been one of my favourite games to date. I was originally inspired to play this from seeing people's creative work in game on social media. 
My experience with this game has been very positive, from the hours I spent playing that felt like minutes, to the amazing community that have helped newbies with their gaming experience. While alot of fun features are behind DLC content, the game is very much worth it. If you are looking for a city sim game, look no further.
If you aren't keen on the DLC or modding aspect, cities:Skylines 2 is another option to consider.

This game is really good. 
Although it is around 13 years old then it still works fine considering Cities Skylines II rating is 48% good.
I recommend to buy the deluxe starter pack because otherwise its a bit boring but for 8€ standard and 16€ deluxe buy it if you don't have a GOD PC.
its a very good game and runs smoothly on my pc and is overall very fun
it's a nice game with alot of customization options you can fiddle with
best game in the genre of simulation games.. if you like city simulations then go for it.

Extremely wonderful game! Unparalleled value at this price-point, it allows you to simulate building a city in one of the most comprehensive manners I have ever experienced. Initially, I was a bit skeptical with it being an 8-year-old game, but my 132 hours of playtime speak for themselves ;) Definitely won't recommend if you cannot afford to get addicted XD

Graphics look way better then on PS5 where I'm kind of experienced. I'm now just getting used to all the new buttons and quick keymapping for on Mac. Very nice relaxed game that's not competitive at all and just a nice spend of time. I spent 200 bucks on all the DLCs I think I need but already enjoy the hell out of it. Countryside and Industries and Pedestrian.. those are awesome.
fantastic city builder. graphics are nice but a bit simplistic. if you liked sim city in the 90s you'll love this.
Good game with amazing modding community. I would recommend this game to anyone who loves needs balancing or city development
When yh Tangerang bisa dibenerin kaya di game ini , WOI ANDRA SONI URUSIN RAJEG JANGAN NGURUSIN BOCAH NYEBAT

When i first played this game on xbox, i made a decent city and all was well. until the sickness attacked. my people would get sick, i would place down healthcare, they wouldnt get better. so i built another city on the other side of the river, and it was paradise amid a restless and dying population. turns out i put the water intake downstream of the sewage output. many lessons were learned that day.

Originally I was using microsoft game pass version due to not even knowing there was a difference. I watched some real game play online and made the switch. Never looking back I lost out on so much over the years before diving into the real game. It runs fine on my low RAM outdated CPU machine even with mods and dlc. worth every bit to get even with new version now out, they have not let this one be over looked.

Huge SimCity fan from the original, 2000, 4.  Cities Skylines is the spiritual successor to that.  That in 2026 there is still significant content updates is also awesome.  I own the game on PS4 and PC.  I can spend way too much time playing this game vanilla, let alone exploring mods.  

My only complaint is new updates are usually poorly tested- many obvious glitches that takes weeks and sometimes more than one patch to get right.

This game is so very good in concept, of course... But it feels HORRIBLE! No game should be this Blurry and Unresponsive.

A metaphor to how this game feels would be having to drive pulling on the wires BY HAND on the Throttle and Clutch. And a foggy windshield. Also in the fog. and Drunk!
